senses.definition: An old scientific term for plants with naked seeds, now usually called gymnosperms, such as conifers.
examples: In older botany books, gymnosperms are sometimes called archisperms.
The professor briefly explained the historical use of the term archisperm in plant classification.

senses.definition: An old taxonomic class name used for gymnosperms, plants whose seeds are not enclosed in an ovary.
examples: The textbook mentioned Archispermae as an early name for gymnosperms.
Archispermae were contrasted with Metaspermae in older systems of plant classification.

senses.definition: A technical term in older biology for the central area of a cell, especially around the centrosome.
examples: Under the microscope, the researcher identified the archisphere near the cell nucleus.
In nineteenth‑century cell theory, the archisphere was carefully described and labeled.

senses.definition: An early or primitive spore in some plants or fungi, used mainly in older scientific writing.
examples: The fossil record showed evidence of an archispore stage.
Some authors used the term archispore when discussing the evolution of spore plants.

senses.definition: In embryology, an old term for the first opening that forms in the early embryo.
examples: The archistome appears at an early stage of embryonic development.
Older zoology texts use the word archistome for what is now more commonly called the blastopore.

senses.definition: Supreme above all others; used rarely and mainly for emphasis.
examples: The king was regarded as the archisupreme authority in the land.
In the story, magic is portrayed as the archisupreme power.

senses.definition: Highly symbolic or representing a basic, central symbol; very rare.
examples: The poet saw the river as an archisymbolical image of life.
In his theory, the circle is an archisymbolical form of perfection.

senses.definition: An old term for the chief ruler of a synagogue in ancient Jewish communities.
examples: In some translations, the leader is called the archisynagogue.
The archisynagogue held both religious and social authority.

senses.definition: A person whose job is to design buildings and often manage their construction.
examples: The architect designed a modern glass office tower.
They hired an architect to plan their new house.

senses.definition: A person who is the main creator of an important plan, system, or event.
examples: She was the chief architect of the company’s new strategy.
History remembers him as one of the architects of the peace agreement.

senses.definition: Designed and structured a complex system, especially a computer or business system.
examples: The team architected a scalable cloud platform.
This network was architected to handle millions of users.

senses.definition: Designing and structuring a complex system, especially in computing or business.
examples: She is architecting the company’s new data platform.
We spent months architecting the overall system before writing code.

senses.definition: Relating to building or structure, or having an organizing or shaping role; very rare.
examples: The essay explored the architective forces behind social change.
He admired the architective power of language in the novel.

senses.definition: Relating to architecture or to the structure and organization of something.
examples: The novel has a clear architectonic design.
They discussed the architectonic principles of the cathedral’s façade.

senses.definition: A rare term relating to architecture or structural design, sometimes used as a title or name.
examples: The artist presented a series called Architectonica.
Architectonica explores the relationship between space and structure.

senses.definition: In a way that concerns structure, organization, or architectural design.
examples: The novel is architectonically complex.
The building is architectonically impressive but difficult to maintain.

senses.definition: The study or systematic organization of the structure of something, especially in art, philosophy, or architecture.
examples: The course in architectonics covered the basic forms of building design.
Kant used the term architectonics to describe the structured system of human knowledge.

senses.definition: An old or rare word for a female architect.
examples: The article from 1900 referred to her as an architectress.
Architectress is now considered dated and is rarely used.

senses.definition: Plural of architect: people whose job is to design buildings and often manage their construction.
examples: The city hired several architects to redesign the downtown area.
Architects must balance safety, cost, and beauty in their work.

senses.definition: Relating to the design and construction of buildings.
examples: The city is famous for its unique architectural style.
They took photos of the architectural details on the old church.

senses.definition: A rare word for a person who is strongly concerned with architectural style or theory.
examples: As an architecturalist, he focused on the harmony of city skylines.
The critic was an architecturalist with a passion for modern design.

senses.definition: In a way that relates to the design and structure of buildings.
examples: The building is architecturally important for the region.
Architecturally, the museum mixes old and new elements.

senses.definition: The art and practice of designing and constructing buildings.
examples: She studied architecture at university.
The city’s architecture attracts many tourists.

senses.definition: The structure of a system or object, especially a computer system or an organization.
examples: They improved the software architecture to make it faster.
The report describes the architecture of the new health‑care system.

senses.definition: Plural of architecture: different building styles or different structures of systems.
examples: Students compared the architectures of European capitals.
The book explains several computer architectures.

senses.definition: Having a picturesque or striking architectural quality; very rare.
examples: The small town is full of architecturesque streets and squares.
They chose an architecturesque setting for the film.

senses.definition: A genus of very large squid, including the giant squid found in deep oceans.
examples: Scientists recently filmed Architeuthis in its natural habitat.
Architeuthis has long fascinated marine biologists.

senses.definition: An old medical term for inflammation of the rectum or anus.
examples: The physician’s notes mentioned a diagnosis of architis.
Architis is no longer a common term in modern medicine.

senses.definition: Relating to or having an architrave.
examples: The room has an architraval doorway.
They restored the architraval mouldings around the windows.

senses.definition: In classical architecture, the main beam resting on the tops of columns.
examples: The stone architrave above the columns had begun to crack.
They studied how the architrave supports the structure of the temple.

senses.definition: A decorative frame or moulding around a door, window, or other opening.
examples: They chose a simple white architrave for the new doors.
The architrave around the window was painted gold.

senses.definition: Having an architrave, especially around doors or windows.
examples: The hall opens through an architraved doorway.
Each window is architraved with carved stone.

senses.definition: Plural of architrave: beams on columns or decorative frames around openings.
examples: The church’s doors have beautifully carved architraves.
Many of the stone architraves had to be replaced.

senses.definition: An old word for the chief steward or head waiter at a feast, especially in ancient times.
examples: In some translations, the architricline tasted the wine first.
The architricline was responsible for the order of the banquet.

senses.definition: A less common spelling of archetype: a typical example of a person or thing, or an original model.
examples: He is the architype of a self‑made entrepreneur.
The story presents the architype of the wise old mentor.

senses.definition: Plural of architype: typical examples or original models of something.
examples: The book discusses several architypes of leadership.
These characters are architypes found in many myths.

senses.definition: A very rare term for someone who prints or writes first models or originals.
examples: The historian referred to him as an architypographer of the text.
An architypographer would prepare the first standard version of a document.

senses.definition: Relating to archives or to the storing of historical records and documents.
examples: The museum has strict archival standards for storage.
They used archival materials to preserve the photographs.

senses.definition: In a way that relates to the proper keeping or treatment of archives.
examples: The documents were archivally preserved.
Archivally, the film is well protected against damage.

senses.definition: A rare architectural term for the curved or arched part of a vault.
examples: The stonework of the archivault was finely carved.
Restoration of the archivault required special skills.

senses.definition: A collection of historical documents or records, or the place where they are stored.
examples: The city archive holds records going back 200 years.
Researchers visited the national archives to study old maps.

senses.definition: A set of stored computer files or data that are kept for future use and not needed often.
examples: You can move old emails into an archive.
The website keeps an online archive of past articles.

senses.definition: To store documents, data, or records so that they can be used again in the future.
examples: The company archives all financial records for ten years.
You should archive those emails instead of deleting them.

senses.definition: Stored for future use and not used regularly, especially about documents or data.
examples: The archived files are kept on a separate server.
You can still read archived articles on the newspaper’s website.

senses.definition: Past tense and past participle of archive: stored something so it can be used again in the future.
examples: They archived the project documents after completion.
I archived the photos to save space on my phone.

senses.definition: A person or program that archives documents or data.
examples: The company hired an archiver to organize its records.
Use an archiver to compress and store the files.

senses.definition: Plural of archiver: people or programs that archive documents or data.
examples: Different archivers may use different file formats.
Museum archivers must handle delicate materials carefully.

senses.definition: Plural of archive: collections or places where historical documents or records are stored.
examples: The state archives are open to the public.
She works in the university archives.

senses.definition: Third person singular of archive: stores documents or data for future use.
examples: The system automatically archives old messages.
The software archives your files every night.

senses.definition: The process of storing documents, records, or data so they can be used again in the future.
examples: Good archiving is essential for any large organization.
The company introduced a new archiving system for emails.

senses.definition: Present participle of archive: storing something so it can be used again later.
examples: We are archiving last year’s financial records.
She is archiving the photos on an external drive.

senses.definition: A person whose job is to collect, organize, preserve, and provide access to historical records and documents.
examples: The archivist helped me find my grandfather’s military records.
Archivists must know how to store fragile documents safely.

senses.definition: Plural of archivist: people whose job is to collect, organize, preserve, and provide access to historical records and documents.
examples: Archivists often work closely with historians.
Many archivists specialize in digital records.

senses.definition: A band of moulding or stonework following the curve of an arch, often decorated.
examples: The cathedral’s main door is framed by a sculpted archivolt.
They carefully cleaned the carvings on the archivolt above the entrance.

senses.definition: Plural of archivolt: curved bands of moulding or stonework around an arch.
examples: The church portal has several richly carved archivolts.
Weather has damaged some of the outer archivolts.

senses.definition: Relating to the earliest geological ages of the Earth, before complex life appeared.
examples: The rocks belong to an archizoic era of the planet’s history.
Scientists studied archizoic formations deep in the crust.
